growth_records.strain: C2H6-fed bioreactor enrichment (dominant 'Ca. A. nitratireducens', ANI 99.96% to C3H8-fed reference genome)
growth_records.medium: Anoxic mineral medium (per ref 16) with nitrate; ethane as sole carbon/energy source

up.method: colorimetric/IC N-species measurement, N balance batch test (Fig 1C, Suppl Table 1)

up.units: μmol consumed per batch (0.6 L vessel)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: electrons to NO3- reduction (denitrification + DNRA) vs max electrons from C2H6 -> CO2
mfa.note: electron balance confirms electrons mainly diverted to nitrate reduction
remainder assimilated into biomass; ethane activated via fumarate addition -> ethyl-succinate (m/z 275>73.1)
closes N balance; final products N2 + NH4+

yields.note: ~29% of ethane C not recovered as CO2 (assimilated to biomass); no numeric Yxs reported

growth_records.cite: Mengxiong Wu et al. Nitrate-driven anaerobic oxidation of ethane and butane by bacteria. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrad011. [PMC10811727]
growth_records.method: not reported (enrichment culture; no biomass-specific growth rate given)
growth_records.snippet: The total amounts of the produced nitrogen species (NH4+ + N2) for C2H6- (2.21 ± 0.15 mmol N/l) ... close to the amounts of nitrogen oxyanions consumed (NO3- + NO2-, 1.98 ± 0.10 ... mmol N/l). ... electrons ... represent 96 ± 2% ... of the
growth_records.notes: Enrichment culture (not pure), dominant organism 'Ca. A. nitratireducens' (bacterium, Symbiobacteriia). All values are volumetric (per L culture) or per-batch amounts, NOT per gDW, because no biomass concentration reported; no growth rate derivable. pH taken as midpoint of stated 6.8-7.5 (=7.15). Temperature 35 ± 1°C. Isotope batch in 0.6 L vessel with 13C2H6 + 15NO3-. Anoxic mineral medium composition referenced to Lai et al. (ref 16), not restated. Ethane oxidation stoichiometry: C2H6 + 7NO3- -> 2CO2 + 7NO2- + 3H2O (ΔG°'=-949 kJ/mol). CO2 fractions: 40 μmol 13CO2 = 67% of 60 μmol consumed 13C; total CO2 71% of consumed C.

growth_records.strain: C4H10-fed bioreactor enrichment (dominant 'Ca. A. nitratireducens', ANI 99.55% to C3H8-fed reference genome)
growth_records.medium: Anoxic mineral medium (per ref 16) with nitrate; n-butane as sole carbon/energy source

up.method: N balance batch test (Fig 1D, Suppl Table 1)

up.units: μmol consumed per batch (0.6 L vessel)

sec.units: mmol/L/day (DNRA rate, butane reactor; significantly higher than ethane, P<0.05)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: electrons to NO3- reduction (denitrification + DNRA) vs max electrons from C4H10 -> CO2
mfa.note: electron balance confirms electrons mainly diverted to nitrate reduction
remainder assimilated into biomass; butane activated via fumarate addition -> butyl-succinate (m/z 303>147.1)
closes N balance; final products N2 + NH4+

yields.note: ~17% of butane C not recovered as CO2 (assimilated to biomass); no numeric Yxs reported

growth_records.cite: Mengxiong Wu et al. Nitrate-driven anaerobic oxidation of ethane and butane by bacteria. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrad011. [PMC10811727]
growth_records.method: not reported (enrichment culture; no biomass-specific growth rate given)
growth_records.snippet: C4H10-fed (1.53 ± 0.08 mmol N/l) batch tests were close to the amounts of nitrogen oxyanions consumed (... 1.63 ± 0.10 mmol N/l ...). ... 99 ± 6% of the maximum electrons available in ... C4H10 oxidation to CO2 ... higher DNRA rates ... in
growth_records.notes: Enrichment culture (not pure), dominant 'Ca. A. nitratireducens' (bacterium). All values volumetric or per-batch, NOT per gDW (no biomass conc.; no growth rate derivable). pH midpoint 7.15 of stated 6.8-7.5; T 35±1°C. Isotope batch 0.6 L with 13C4H10 + 15NO3-. Butane stoichiometry: C4H10 + 13NO3- -> 4CO2 + 13NO2- + 5H2O (ΔG°'=-1752 kJ/mol). CO2 fractions: 661 μmol 13CO2 = 77% of 840 μmol consumed 13C; total CO2 83%. Same species also shown metabolically versatile (oxidizes ethane, propane, butane) in substrate-range tests.
